title: Ambassador Douglas E. Lute Named Senior Fellow
summary: Former NATO Ambassador Douglas E. Lute joined the Belfer Center in April as a Senior Fellow with the Center’s Future of Diplomacy Project.
body: Former NATO Ambassador Douglas E. Lute joined the Belfer Center in April as a Senior Fellow with the Center’s Future of Diplomacy Project. At the Kennedy School, Lute will initiate a research project focused on NATO and transatlantic relations that will address the multiplicity of challenges facing the Alliance as it approaches its 70th anniversary, said Nicholas Burns , Faculty Director of the Future of Diplomacy Project. Lute will also share his expertise in security and diplomacy by conducting seminars and study groups with students and fellows. “I am proud to welcome Ambassador Doug Lute to the Kennedy School’s Future of Diplomacy Project,” Burns said. “As a former U.S. Ambassador to NATO myself, I look forward to working with him on a project to strengthen our vital NATO alliance and transatlantic ties.” Appointed by President Barack Obama , Lute served as the United States Permanent Representative to NATO from 2013-2017. During this period, he was instrumental in designing and implementing the Alliance’s responses to the most severe security challenges in Europe since the end of the Cold War. He received the State Department’s Distinguished Honor Award for his work. A career Army officer, Lute retired from active duty in 2010 as a Lieutenant General after 35 years of service. In 2007, President George W. Bush named him Assistant to the President and Deputy National Security Advisor to coordinate the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan. Retained by President Obama to focus on South Asia, he served six years in the White House. “Doug has served the nation with distinction under two administrations and before that with 35 years of military service,” said Belfer Center Director Graham Allison . “It’s an honor to have such a distinguished public servant join the Center and share his experience and insights with the Harvard community.” The latest from Belfer See more Recommended citation "Ambassador Douglas E. result.main_content_en: Former U.S. Ambassador to NATO Douglas E. Lute has joined Harvard Kennedy School's Belfer Center as a Senior Fellow with the Future of Diplomacy Project. He will lead a research initiative examining NATO and transatlantic relations, focusing on the multifaceted challenges confronting the Alliance as it nears its 70th anniversary. Ambassador Lute, appointed by President Obama, served as the U.S. Permanent Representative to NATO from 2013 to 2017, where he played a key role in formulating the Alliance's response to post-Cold War Europe's most significant security crises, earning the State Department's Distinguished Honor Award. His extensive background includes a 35-year Army career, retiring as a Lieutenant General, and serving six years in the White House under Presidents Bush and Obama as Deputy National Security Advisor, coordinating wars in Iraq and Afghanistan and later focusing on South Asia. At the Belfer Center, he will conduct seminars and share his diplomatic and security expertise with students and fellows.
result.main_content_zh: 前美国驻北约大使道格拉斯·E·卢特已加入哈佛大学肯尼迪学院贝尔弗中心，担任其“外交未来项目”的高级研究员。他将主导一项关于北约及跨大西洋关系的研究项目，重点探讨该联盟在临近成立70周年之际所面临的多重挑战。卢特大使由奥巴马总统任命，于2013年至2017年担任美国驻北约常驻代表，期间他在制定联盟应对冷战后欧洲最严峻安全危机的策略方面发挥了关键作用，并因此获得了国务院的卓越荣誉奖。他拥有35年的陆军生涯，以中将军衔退役，并在布什和奥巴马总统任内于白宫担任了六年的总统助理兼副国家安全顾问，负责协调伊拉克和阿富汗战争，后专注于南亚事务。在贝尔弗中心，他将举办研讨会，并与学生和研究员分享其外交与安全领域的专业知识。
